summary.overall_regression: Critical regression: health score 45 → 40 (+5 pts). 2 critical incident(s).
summary.causal_chain_text: 'free buffer waits' (58.9% DB time) → 'buffer busy waits' (6.5% DB time) → 'enq: FB - contention' (8.2% DB time) → 'buffer busy waits' (6.5% DB time) | 'enq: US - contention' (1.2% DB time)
summary.headline: DB Time rose 76% driven by: 7 new application SQL(s) (top: 9huj1akp5b757, 41576s).
headline_evidence: Both periods are Concurrency-bound (bottleneck type unchanged)
7 SQL(s) regressed without plan change (+824437s cumulative)
Top worsened wait: 'free buffer waits' 12.5%→58.9% DB Time

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Server process cannot find a free buffer in the buffer cache. DBWR is posted to write dirty buffers to disk but cannot keep up. Causes cascading contention — other sessions also queue for free buffers.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Server process cannot find a free buffer in the buffer cache. DBWR is posted to write dirty buffers to disk but cannot keep up. Causes cascading contention — other sessions also queue for free buffers.
pathology_investigate: DBWR I/O throughput (db file parallel write avg wait)
heavy DML workload generating dirty buffers

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Format Block enqueue contention — sessions waiting to format new blocks in the buffer cache. Related to free buffer pressure: when DBWR can't free buffers fast enough, sessions contend on this enqueue while trying to allocate buffer space.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Format Block enqueue contention — sessions waiting to format new blocks in the buffer cache. Related to free buffer pressure: when DBWR can't free buffers fast enough, sessions contend on this enqueue while trying to allocate buffer space.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
pathology_investigate: top segments by buffer busy waits

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
pathology_investigate: missing indexes causing unselective index scans

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Session waiting for free space in the log buffer. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough, or log_buffer is too small for the redo generation rate.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Session waiting for free space in the log buffer. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough, or log_buffer is too small for the redo generation rate.
pathology_investigate: LGWR write latency (log file parallel write)

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Undo Segment contention — sessions waiting for undo segment resources. Can indicate undo tablespace is too small or too many concurrent transactions.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Undo Segment contention — sessions waiting for undo segment resources. Can indicate undo tablespace is too small or too many concurrent transactions.
pathology_investigate: undo tablespace usage and free space

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.
pathology_investigate: large result sets in top SQL (rows/exec)

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Redo allocation latch contention — high commit rate or large redo generation causing sessions to queue for redo allocation.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Redo allocation latch contention — high commit rate or large redo generation causing sessions to queue for redo allocation.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Redo copy latch — sessions waiting to copy redo into the log buffer. Indicates redo generation exceeds copy latch capacity.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Redo copy latch — sessions waiting to copy redo into the log buffer. Indicates redo generation exceeds copy latch capacity.

new_bottlenecks.root_cause_hint: Undo Segment contention — sessions waiting for undo segment resources. Can indicate undo tablespace is too small or too many concurrent transactions.
new_bottlenecks.pathology_meaning: Undo Segment contention — sessions waiting for undo segment resources. Can indicate undo tablespace is too small or too many concurrent transactions.
pathology_investigate: undo tablespace usage and free space

new_bottlenecks.root_cause_hint: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.

sql_regressions.sql_text_truncated: DECLARE c_job_name CONSTANT VARCHAR2(50) :='ff_skuproj_daily_pre'; v_errcode VARCHAR2(50); v_errdesc VARCHAR2(1000); v_start PLS_INTEGER; v_end PLS_INTEGER; v_min PLS_INTEGER; v_sec PLS_INTEGER; v_vol
sql_regressions.sql_text_full: DECLARE c_job_name CONSTANT VARCHAR2(50) :='ff_skuproj_daily_pre'; v_errcode VARCHAR2(50); v_errdesc VARCHAR2(1000); v_start PLS_INTEGER; v_end PLS_INTEGER; v_min PLS_INTEGER; v_sec PLS_INTEGER; v_volume NUMBER; v_err PLS_INTEGER; v_ohpost DATE; v_calstart DATE; v_caladj PLS_INTEGER; BEGIN :RC := 0; --Update Eff on caldata for calendar SKUPROJ_ONLY --SELECT TO_DATE(param_value) INTO v_ohpost SELECT TRUNC(TO_DATE(param_value), 'IW') INTO v_ohpost FROM u_system_param WHERE param_name='OHPOST'; SELECT TO_DATE('01011970', 'DDMMYYYY') + MIN(eff)/(24*60) INTO v_calstart FROM scpomgr.caldata WHERE cal='SKUPROJ_ONLY'; --IF TRUNC(v_calstart) < TRUNC(v_ohpost) THEN IF TRUNC(v_calstart) != TRUNC(v_ohpost) THEN v_caladj := TRUNC(v_ohpost) - TRUNC(v_calstart); UPDATE SCP OMGR.CALDATA SET EFF=(((TO_DATE('01011970', 'DDMMYYYY') + eff/(24*60) + v_caladj) - TO_DATE('01011970', 'DDMMYYYY')) * 1440) WHERE CAL='SKUPROJ_ONLY'; pkg_log.info('SKUPROJ_ONLY Calendar rows updated: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); COMMIT; ELSE pkg_log.info('SKUPROJ_ONLY Calendar is current'); END IF; scpomgr.pkg_ddl.truncate_table('PROCESSSKU'); scpomgr.pkg_ddl.truncate_table('UDT_SKUPROJSTATIC'); scpomgr.pkg_ddl.truncate_table('SKUSTATSTATIC'); EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN pkg_job.fatal_error; :RC := '1'; ROLLBACK; v_errdesc := SUBSTR(SQLERRM, 1, 1000); v_errcode := TO_CHAR(SQLCODE); pkg_log.error(c_job_name||' ORACLE ERROR -'||v_errcode||' '||v_errdesc); END;

recommendations.action: Use Real-Time SQL Monitoring for execution-level visibility. Compare plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR('<sql_id>')); Gather fresh statistics or use SQL Plan Baseline to lock good plan.

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'free buffer waits' classified as worsening: 84000.0s -> 698500.0s (+671%) [Volume increase — stable latency, more waits]
recommendations.action: Server process cannot find a free buffer in the buffer cache. DBWR is posted to write dirty buffers to disk but cannot keep up. Causes cascading contention — other sessions also queue for free buffers.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'free buffer waits';
recommendations.impact: 58.9% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Hot block waits (buffer busy waits) total 6.5% DB time (was 1.6%). Indicates hot index/table blocks — monotonic keys or block-level row clustering.
recommendations.action: Check V$SEGSTAT / DBA_HIST_SEG_STAT for segments with high buffer_busy_waits. Consider reverse-key indexes for monotonic columns. Use DBMS_SPACE.OBJECT_SPACE_USAGE to find hot objects.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: 'log buffer space' waits = 0.70% DB time. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough — sessions stall writing redo entries.
recommendations.action: Increase LOG_BUFFER init parameter (default 3–8 MB; try 32–64 MB for write-heavy workloads). Check redo log storage latency. Ensure redo logs are on separate fast storage.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: Undo-related contention detected (1.2% DB time).  Long readers against changing data risk ORA-01555 snapshot too old errors.
recommendations.action: Check UNDO_RETENTION vs query duration. Run: SELECT name, value FROM v$parameter WHERE name LIKE 'undo%'. Review DBA_UNDO_EXTENTS for shrinkage under pressure.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: 'free buffer waits' is a 2.6σ statistical outlier: 58.9% of DB time (baseline mean: 8.0%). This event is anomalously dominant in the bad period.
recommendations.action: Server process cannot find a free buffer in the buffer cache. DBWR is posted to write dirty buffers to disk but cannot keep up. Causes cascading contention — other sessions also queue for free buffers.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'enq: FB - contention' classified as worsening: 14600.0s -> 97600.0s (+520%) [Both volume and latency increased]
recommendations.action: Format Block enqueue contention — sessions waiting to format new blocks in the buffer cache. Related to free buffer pressure: when DBWR can't free buffers fast enough, sessions contend on this enqueue while trying to allocate buffer space.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'enq: FB - contention';
recommendations.impact: 8.2% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'buffer busy waits' classified as worsening: 11000.0s -> 77000.0s (+549%) [Both volume and latency increased]
recommendations.action: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'buffer busy waits';
recommendations.impact: 6.5% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'db file sequential read' classified as worsening: 4994.2s -> 34800.0s (+546%) [Both volume and latency increased]
recommendations.action: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'db file sequential read';
recommendations.impact: 2.9% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'log buffer space' classified as worsening: 3422.6s -> 8880.7s (+140%)
recommendations.action: Session waiting for free space in the log buffer. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough, or log_buffer is too small for the redo generation rate.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'log buffer space';
recommendations.impact: 0.7% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'enq: US - contention';
recommendations.impact: 1.2% of DB time in bad period

incident_indicators.diagnostic_sql: SELECT object_name, object_type, value FROM v$segstat s JOIN dba_objects o ON s.obj# = o.object_id WHERE statistic_name = 'buffer busy waits' ORDER BY value DESC FETCH FIRST 10 ROWS ONLY;

incident_indicators.description: 'log buffer space' waits = 0.70% DB time. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough — sessions stall writing redo entries.
incident_indicators.remediation: Increase LOG_BUFFER init parameter (default 3–8 MB; try 32–64 MB for write-heavy workloads). Check redo log storage latency. Ensure redo logs are on separate fast storage.
incident_indicators.diagnostic_sql: SELECT name, value FROM v$sysstat WHERE name IN ('redo log space requests', 'redo buffer allocation retries');

incident_indicators.description: Undo-related contention detected (1.2% DB time).  Long readers against changing data risk ORA-01555 snapshot too old errors.
incident_indicators.remediation: Check UNDO_RETENTION vs query duration. Run: SELECT name, value FROM v$parameter WHERE name LIKE 'undo%'. Review DBA_UNDO_EXTENTS for shrinkage under pressure.
incident_indicators.diagnostic_sql: SELECT tablespace_name, status, SUM(bytes)/1048576 AS mb FROM dba_undo_extents GROUP BY tablespace_name, status ORDER BY status;

incident_indicators.description: 12 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL fzqdq3nxzjnz9 — 153195s → 940107s (+469%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
incident_indicators.remediation: Use Real-Time SQL Monitoring for execution-level visibility. Compare plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR('<sql_id>')); Gather fresh statistics or use SQL Plan Baseline to lock good plan.
incident_indicators.diagnostic_sql: SELECT sql_id, plan_hash_value, executions, elapsed_time/1e6 AS elapsed_s, cpu_time/1e6 AS cpu_s FROM dba_hist_sqlstat WHERE sql_id = 'fzqdq3nxzjnz9' ORDER BY snap_id DESC;

incident_indicators.description: 'free buffer waits' is a 2.6σ statistical outlier: 58.9% of DB time (baseline mean: 8.0%). This event is anomalously dominant in the bad period.
incident_indicators.remediation: Server process cannot find a free buffer in the buffer cache. DBWR is posted to write dirty buffers to disk but cannot keep up. Causes cascading contention — other sessions also queue for free buffers.

all_metrics.label: DB Time/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Total database work per second. Core workload intensity metric.

all_metrics.label: DB CPU/sec
all_metrics.explanation: CPU consumed by database per second. Increasing means more compute demand.

all_metrics.label: Logical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer gets per second. High values indicate intensive SQL scanning.

all_metrics.label: Physical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk read blocks per second. High value with low Read IO Requests/s = large multi-block full-table scans (direct-path, invisible to Segments section). High value with proportionally high Read IO Requests/s = many single-block index reads. Compare both metrics together.

all_metrics.label: Physical Writes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk writes per second. Increase may indicate more DML or redo/undo activity.

all_metrics.label: Redo Size/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Bytes of redo generated per second. Higher means more DML workload.

all_metrics.label: Hard Parses/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Hard parses per second. >100/s indicates literal SQL or cursor invalidation.

all_metrics.label: Executes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions per second. Application throughput indicator.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User commits+rollbacks per second. Primary business throughput KPI.

all_metrics.label: Logons/sec
all_metrics.explanation: New session creations per second. Spike signals connection pool churn.

all_metrics.label: User Calls/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User-originated calls per second. Overall application request rate.

all_metrics.label: Block Changes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer blocks modified per second. Correlates with redo and DML volume.

all_metrics.label: Buffer Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Data found in RAM vs disk. <95% = excessive physical I/O.

all_metrics.label: Library Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL look-ups that found the cursor in memory. Low = shared pool too small.

all_metrics.label: Soft Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions that reused a cached plan. <95% = hard parse storm.

all_metrics.label: Execute to Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: % executes reusing open cursor. Low = cursor caching not enabled.

all_metrics.label: Latch Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Internal Oracle lock efficiency. <99% = contention in shared memory.

all_metrics.label: DB Time (min)
all_metrics.explanation: Total database active time. Core load indicator.

all_metrics.label: Avg Active Sessions
all_metrics.explanation: Sessions actively working. >CPU count = saturation.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Business throughput KPI (commits+rollbacks per second).

all_metrics.label: Elapsed Window
all_metrics.explanation: Observation window length. Large difference = unequal comparison.

all_metrics.label: free buffer waits
all_metrics.explanation: Server process cannot find a free buffer in the buffer cache. DBWR is posted to write dirty buffers to disk but cannot keep up. Causes cascading contention — other sessions also queue for free buffers.

all_metrics.label: enq: FB - contention
all_metrics.explanation: Format Block enqueue contention — sessions waiting to format new blocks in the buffer cache. Related to free buffer pressure: when DBWR can't free buffers fast enough, sessions contend on this enqueue while trying to allocate buffer space.

all_metrics.label: buffer busy waits
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.

all_metrics.label: db file sequential read
all_metrics.explanation: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.

all_metrics.label: log buffer space
all_metrics.explanation: Session waiting for free space in the log buffer. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough, or log_buffer is too small for the redo generation rate.

all_metrics.label: enq: US - contention
all_metrics.explanation: Undo Segment contention — sessions waiting for undo segment resources. Can indicate undo tablespace is too small or too many concurrent transactions.

all_metrics.label: enq: TX - index contention
all_metrics.explanation: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.

recommendations.finding: Log file sync average wait is 124.9ms, exceeding the 10ms threshold.  Commit latency is elevated.
recommendations.action: Move redo logs to faster storage or reduce commit frequency
recommendations.oracle_fix:
-- Move redo logs to SSD/flash
-- Consider COMMIT WRITE BATCH NOWAIT for non-critical transactions
recommendations.impact: Reduces commit latency and session queuing

recommendations.finding: CPU saturation detected: AAS=651.1 exceeds CPU count 36 x 1.5.
recommendations.action: Identify top CPU SQL, reduce parallelism, or scale CPU
recommendations.oracle_fix:
-- Identify top CPU consumers:
SELECT sql_id, cpu_time/1e6 cpu_s FROM v$sqlstats ORDER BY cpu_time DESC FETCH FIRST 10 ROWS ONLY;
recommendations.impact: Reduces response time and prevents runaway queueing

analysis_notes: RULE: ADDM recommendations are guides, not gospel. Validate each recommendation against multiple AWR periods before making system changes. Do NOT increase SGA/PGA based on a single AWR report alone — gather at least 3–5 representative periods.
RULE: AWR averages hide spikes. A 60-minute snapshot can mask a 5-minute crisis. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports or use ASH.
RULE: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio is not a primary health signal. A 99% hit ratio can coexist with a burning system. Investigate wait events and per-execution SQL metrics instead.

investigation_trail.finding: AAS = 398.7 vs 36 CPUs — database severely overloaded, OS CPU busy: 56%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Database is doing 2x more work than CPU capacity. Every other metric matters.

investigation_trail.conclusion: Primary bottleneck category: COMMIT. Next: find Top SQL by Elapsed that links to this wait, then check Segment Statistics to confirm which object is under pressure.

investigation_trail.finding: Top SQL fzqdq3nxzjnz9 at 23% DB time — SQL fzqdq3nxzjnz9: 751,784 execs at 0.204s/exec — high volume but low per-exec cost. Total elapsed is high due to frequency, not because the query is slow. Reduce call frequency or cache results.
investigation_trail.conclusion: RULE: Always evaluate per-execution cost, not just totals. High total elapsed from many cheap calls = volume problem. High per-exec cost from few calls = broken query. Check execution plans for full-table scans, missing indexes, and bind variable usage.

sql_stats.sql_text: DECLARE c_job_name CONSTANT VARCHAR2(50) :='ff_skuproj_daily_pre'; v_errcode VARCHAR2(50); v_errdesc VARCHAR2(1000); v_start PLS_INTEGER; v_end PLS_INTEGER; v_min PLS_INTEGER; v_sec PLS_INTEGER; v_volume NUMBER; v_err PLS_INTEGER; v_ohpost DATE; v_calstart DATE; v_caladj PLS_INTEGER; BEGIN :RC := 0; --Update Eff on caldata for calendar SKUPROJ_ONLY --SELECT TO_DATE(param_value) INTO v_ohpost SELECT TRUNC(TO_DATE(param_value), 'IW') INTO v_ohpost FROM u_system_param WHERE param_name='OHPOST'; SELECT TO_DATE('01011970', 'DDMMYYYY') + MIN(eff)/(24*60) INTO v_calstart FROM scpomgr.caldata WHERE cal='SKUPROJ_ONLY'; --IF TRUNC(v_calstart) < TRUNC(v_ohpost) THEN IF TRUNC(v_calstart) != TRUNC(v_ohpost) THEN v_caladj := TRUNC(v_ohpost) - TRUNC(v_calstart); UPDATE SCP OMGR.CALDATA SET EFF=(((TO_DATE('01011970', 'DDMMYYYY') + eff/(24*60) + v_caladj) - TO_DATE('01011970', 'DDMMYYYY')) * 1440) WHERE CAL='SKUPROJ_ONLY'; pkg_log.info('SKUPROJ_ONLY Calendar rows updated: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); COMMIT; ELSE pkg_log.info('SKUPROJ_ONLY Calendar is current'); END IF; scpomgr.pkg_ddl.truncate_table('PROCESSSKU'); scpomgr.pkg_ddl.truncate_table('UDT_SKUPROJSTATIC'); scpomgr.pkg_ddl.truncate_table('SKUSTATSTATIC'); EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN pkg_job.fatal_error; :RC := '1'; ROLLBACK; v_errdesc := SUBSTR(SQLERRM, 1, 1000); v_errcode := TO_CHAR(SQLCODE); pkg_log.error(c_job_name||' ORACLE ERROR -'||v_errcode||' '||v_errdesc); END;

CONST_VIEW

1shhygvj3zw36.fullText: SELECT M.APPLICATION_NAME, M.MEASURE_ID FROM MD_MEASURE_APPLICATION M WHERE M.MEASURE_ID = :1

2d6z48thhy80q.fullText: SELECT /* PlanProxyDemandTVQHint */ planproxyinventory.altitem, planproxyinventory.loc, planproxyinventory.proxystartdate, planproxyinventory.proxyenddate, planproxyinventory.primaryitemqty, planproxyinventory.proxydmdreqexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, PLANPROXYINVENTORY WHERE ( PLANPROXYINVENTORY.ALTITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND PLANPROXYINVENTORY.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY planproxyinventory.altitem, planproxyinventory.loc, planproxyinventory.proxystartdate, planproxyinventory.proxyenddate ASC

2h7u1gwu558r7.fullText: INSERT INTO PROCESSSKU ( PROCESSID, ITEM, LOC, ITEMLEVEL, SKULEVEL, NETCHGSW, BATCHNUM ) VALUES ( :B4 , :B1 , :B2 , 0, 0, 1, :B3 )

2qjq3d3u54ajq.fullText: SELECT 1 FROM GV$SESSION A WHERE SID = :B3 AND SERIAL# = :B2 AND INST_ID = :B1 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM MANUGISTICS_SCHEMA B WHERE A.USERNAME = B.SCHEMA_NAME AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYS' AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME <> 'JDA_SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME IS NOT NULL)

4qguf81v4r5mx.fullText: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B4 , STOP_REQUEST_DATE = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), NODE_STATE = :B3 WHERE NODE_STATE = :B2 AND ROWNUM = 1 AND SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID = :B1 RETURNING NODE_ID INTO :O0

5dqz0hqtp9fru.fullText: select /*+ connect_by_filtering index(sysauth$ i_sysauth1) */ privilege#, bitand(nvl(option$, 0), 72), grantee#, level from sysauth$ connect by grantee#=prior privilege# and privilege#>0 start with grantee#=:1 and privilege#>0

63gwvy9mhju8k.fullText: SELECT /*+ ORDERED USE_NL(PROCESSSKU SKU) INDEX (PROCESSSKU PROCESSSKU_PERF) INDEX (SKU SKU_PK) INDEX (LOC LOC_PK) */ /* SKUBatchRetrievalHint */ sku.item item, sku.loc loc, replentype , (case when sku.ohpost > to_date('01/02/1970', 'mm/dd/yyyy') then sku.ohpost else loc.ohpost end) trueohpost FROM processsku, SKU sku, LOC loc WHERE sku.item = processsku.item and sku.loc = processsku.loc AND processsku.loc = loc.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2

6jb71hzd26m1u.fullText: SELECT /* SafetyStockPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc, sspresentation.displayqty, sspresentation.dmdgroup, sspresentation.presentationqty FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC

7j17vjtnkzn98.fullText: SELECT /* TimePhasedProfileRetrievalHint */ DISTINCT spp.stockoutlvl as stockOutLevel, spp.item as item, spp.loc as loc FROM processsku, SKUPlanningParam spp WHERE processsku.item = spp.item and processsku.loc = spp.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3

865qwpcdyggkk.fullText: select spare6 from user$ where user#=:1

8xgs7k1v2db52.fullText: SELECT /*+ LEADING(PROCESSSKU) USE_NL(vehicleload vehicleloadline) INDEX(processsku PROCESSSKU_PERF) */ /* IntransOutTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.source, vehicleloadline.schedshipdate, vehicleload.shipdate, vehicleloadline.expdate, vehicleload.sourcestatus, vehicleloadline.actionallowedsw, vehicleloadline.dmdexpdate, vehicleloadline.primaryitem, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.reviseddate, vehicleloadline.revisedexpdate, vehicleloadline.revisedshipdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.SOURCE=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.source, vehicleloadline.schedshipdate, vehicleload.shipdate, vehicleloadline.exp date ASC

9zg9qd9bm4spu.fullText: update user$ set spare6=DECODE(to_char(:2, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), '0000-00-00', to_date(NULL), :2) where user#=:1

a6tydqr67a7nu.fullText: SELECT SID, SERIAL#, SYS_CONTEXT('userenv', 'instance') FROM V$SESSION WHERE USERNAME = UPPER(:B2 ) AND AUDSID = TO_NUMBER(:B1 )

awh9nk7p0r3v0.fullText: SELECT /*+ LEADING(PROCESSSKU) USE_NL(vehicleload vehicleloadline) INDEX(processsku PROCESSSKU_PERF) */ /* IntransInTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate, vehicleload.deststatus, vehicleload.sourcestatus, vehicleloadline.actionallowedsw, vehicleloadline.expdate, vehicleloadline.primaryitem, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.reviseddate, vehicleloadline.revisedexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate ASC

cg1ukabw429d9.fullText: SELECT /* PlanArrivTVQHint */ planarriv.item, planarriv.dest, planarriv.schedarrivdate, planarriv.expdate, planarriv.actionallowedsw, planarriv.firmplansw, planarriv.qty, planarriv.reviseddate, planarriv.revisedexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, PLANARRIV WHERE ( PLANARRIV.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND PLANARRIV.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY planarriv.item, planarriv.dest, planarriv.schedarrivdate, planarriv.expdate ASC

cxv29ppvmztwv.fullText: SELECT /* IntransInProxyTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE (( vehicleload.deststatus != 3 and vehicleloadline.primaryitem != vehicleloadline.item)) AND ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate ASC

d68fgmq7cuhp7.fullText: delete /* SkuStatStaticPurgeHint */ from SKUSTATSTATIC where (item, loc) in (select item, loc from ProcessSku where ProcessID = :1 and batchnum = :2 )

d9q2q82g1tuzx.fullText: select decode(upper(failover_method), NULL, 0 , 'BASIC', 1, 'PRECONNECT', 2 , 'PREPARSE', 4 , 0), decode(upper(failover_type), NULL, 1, 'NONE', 1, 'SESSION', 2, 'SELECT', 4, 'TRANSACTION', 8, 'AUTO' , 32, 1 ), failover_retries, failover_delay, flags, nvl(replay_initiation_timeout, 900), decode(upper(session_state_consistency), 'STATIC', 16, 0), failover_restore from sys.service$ where name = :1

dn3bb4r318utv.fullText: SELECT /* HoldOutPrimitiveTVQHint */ suppordersku.item, suppordersku.loc, suppordersku.supporderid, suppordersku.holdoutreleasestart, suppordersku.needarrivdate, suppordersku.needqty, suppordersku.type FROM PROCESSSKU, SUPPORDERSKU WHERE ( SUPPORDERSKU.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SUPPORDERSKU.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY suppordersku.item, suppordersku.loc, suppordersku.supporderid ASC

f0h5rpzmhju11.fullText: select SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'SERVER_HOST'), SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'DB_UNIQUE_NAME'), SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'INSTANCE_NAME'), SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'INSTANCE'), STARTUP_TIME, SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'DB_DOMAIN'), SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'SERVICE_NAME') from v$instance

f2f3m4avdtmyc.fullText: SELECT /* SKUControlDataRetrievalHint */ SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.MAXCOVDUR, SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM as SKUITEM , SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC as SKULOC FROM SCPOMGR.SKU, SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM, SCPOMGR.PROCESSSKU where SCPOMGR.PROCESSSKU.PROCESSID = :1 AND SCPOMGR.PROCESSSKU.BATCHNUM = :2 and SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM=SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.ITEM AND SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC=SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.LOC and SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM=SCPOMGR.PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC=SCPOMGR.PROCESSSKU.LOC

_sql_text_map.0ctk7jpux5chm: WITH RECINFO AS ( SELECT /*+ NO_MERGE */ FINDING_ID, COUNT(R.REC_ID) RECCOUNT, R.TYPE FROM DBA_ADVISOR_RECOMMENDATIONS R WHERE TASK_ID=:B1 GROUP BY R.FINDING_ID, R.TYPE ), F_CURR AS ( SELECT /*+ NO_MERGE */ DISTINCT FINDING_NAME FROM DBA_ADVISOR_FINDINGS WHERE TASK_ID=:B1 ), TASKS AS ( SELECT /*+ NO_MERGE */ T.TASK_ID, I.LOCAL_TASK_ID, T.END_TIME, T.BEGIN_TIME FROM DBA_ADDM_TASKS T, DBA_ADDM_INSTANCES I WHERE T.END_TIME>SYSDATE -1 AND T.TASK_ID=I.TASK_ID AND I.INSTANCE_NUMBER=SYS_CONTEXT('USERENV', 'INSTANCE') AND T.REQUESTED_ANALYSIS='INSTANCE' ), F_ALL AS ( SELECT /*+ NO_MERGE */ COUNT(FA.TASK_ID) FINDING_COUNT, FA.FINDING_NAME FROM TASKS, DBA_ADVISOR_FINDINGS FA WHERE FA.TASK_ID=TASKS.TASK_ID AND FA.TYPE<>'INFORMATION' AND FA.TYPE<>'WARNING' AND FA.PARENT=0 GROUP BY FA.FINDING_NAME ), HISTORY AS ( SELECT /*+ NO_MERGE */ FINDIN G_NAME, FINDING_COUNT FROM F_ALL JOIN F_CURR USING (FINDING_NAME) ) SELECT :B1 TASK_ID, F.FINDING_ID FINDING_ID, DECODE(RECINFO.TYPE, NULL, 'Uncategorized', RECINFO.TYPE) REC_TYPE, RECINFO.RECCOUNT REC_COUNT, F.PERC_ACTIVE_SESS IMPACT_PCT, F.MESSAGE MESSAGE, TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(:B3 , 'MM-DD-YYYY HH24:MI:SS'), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') START_TIME, TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(:B2 , 'MM-DD-YYYY HH24:MI:SS'), 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') END_TIME, HISTORY.FINDING_COUNT FINDING_COUNT, F.FINDING_NAME FINDING_NAME, F.ACTIVE_SESSIONS ACTIVE_SESSIONS FROM DBA_ADDM_FINDINGS F, RECINFO, HISTORY WHERE F.TASK_ID=:B1 AND F.TYPE<>'INFORMATION' AND F.TYPE<>'WARNING' AND F.FILTERED<>'Y' AND F.PARENT=0 AND F.FINDING_ID=RECINFO.FINDING_ID (+) AND F.FINDING_NAME=HISTORY.FINDING_NAME ORDER BY F.FINDING_ID

_sql_text_map.0wsr131x9ufsj: DECLARE c_job_name CONSTANT VARCHAR2(50) :='ff_skuproj_daily_pre'; v_errcode VARCHAR2(50); v_errdesc VARCHAR2(1000); v_start PLS_INTEGER; v_end PLS_INTEGER; v_min PLS_INTEGER; v_sec PLS_INTEGER; v_volume NUMBER; v_err PLS_INTEGER; v_ohpost DATE; v_calstart DATE; v_caladj PLS_INTEGER; BEGIN :RC := 0; --Update Eff on caldata for calendar SKUPROJ_ONLY --SELECT TO_DATE(param_value) INTO v_ohpost SELECT TRUNC(TO_DATE(param_value), 'IW') INTO v_ohpost FROM u_system_param WHERE param_name='OHPOST'; SELECT TO_DATE('01011970', 'DDMMYYYY') + MIN(eff)/(24*60) INTO v_calstart FROM scpomgr.caldata WHERE cal='SKUPROJ_ONLY'; --IF TRUNC(v_calstart) < TRUNC(v_ohpost) THEN IF TRUNC(v_calstart) != TRUNC(v_ohpost) THEN v_caladj := TRUNC(v_ohpost) - TRUNC(v_calstart); UPDATE SCP OMGR.CALDATA SET EFF=(((TO_DATE('01011970', 'DDMMYYYY') + eff/(24*60) + v_caladj) - TO_DATE('01011970', 'DDMMYYYY')) * 1440) WHERE CAL='SKUPROJ_ONLY'; pkg_log.info('SKUPROJ_ONLY Calendar rows updated: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); COMMIT; ELSE pkg_log.info('SKUPROJ_ONLY Calendar is current'); END IF; scpomgr.pkg_ddl.truncate_table('PROCESSSKU'); scpomgr.pkg_ddl.truncate_table('UDT_SKUPROJSTATIC'); scpomgr.pkg_ddl.truncate_table('SKUSTATSTATIC'); EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN pkg_job.fatal_error; :RC := '1'; ROLLBACK; v_errdesc := SUBSTR(SQLERRM, 1, 1000); v_errcode := TO_CHAR(SQLCODE); pkg_log.error(c_job_name||' ORACLE ERROR -'||v_errcode||' '||v_errdesc); END;
_sql_text_map.0x21zh4mq0pgq: SELECT /*+ LEADING( PROCESSSKU) USE_NL(processsku vehicleload vehicleloadline) INDEX(processsku PROCESSSKU_PERF) */ /* IntransInSubstTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.primaryitem, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate, vehicleload.deststatus, vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.primaryitemqty FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.PRIMARYITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.primaryitem, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate ASC

_sql_text_map.1shhygvj3zw36: SELECT M.APPLICATION_NAME, M.MEASURE_ID FROM MD_MEASURE_APPLICATION M WHERE M.MEASURE_ID = :1
_sql_text_map.2d6z48thhy80q: SELECT /* PlanProxyDemandTVQHint */ planproxyinventory.altitem, planproxyinventory.loc, planproxyinventory.proxystartdate, planproxyinventory.proxyenddate, planproxyinventory.primaryitemqty, planproxyinventory.proxydmdreqexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, PLANPROXYINVENTORY WHERE ( PLANPROXYINVENTORY.ALTITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND PLANPROXYINVENTORY.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY planproxyinventory.altitem, planproxyinventory.loc, planproxyinventory.proxystartdate, planproxyinventory.proxyenddate ASC
_sql_text_map.2h7u1gwu558r7: INSERT INTO PROCESSSKU ( PROCESSID, ITEM, LOC, ITEMLEVEL, SKULEVEL, NETCHGSW, BATCHNUM ) VALUES ( :B4 , :B1 , :B2 , 0, 0, 1, :B3 )
_sql_text_map.2qjq3d3u54ajq: SELECT 1 FROM GV$SESSION A WHERE SID = :B3 AND SERIAL# = :B2 AND INST_ID = :B1 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM MANUGISTICS_SCHEMA B WHERE A.USERNAME = B.SCHEMA_NAME AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYS' AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME <> 'JDA_SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME IS NOT NULL)
_sql_text_map.4qguf81v4r5mx: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B4 , STOP_REQUEST_DATE = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), NODE_STATE = :B3 WHERE NODE_STATE = :B2 AND ROWNUM = 1 AND SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID = :B1 RETURNING NODE_ID INTO :O0
_sql_text_map.5dqz0hqtp9fru: select /*+ connect_by_filtering index(sysauth$ i_sysauth1) */ privilege#, bitand(nvl(option$, 0), 72), grantee#, level from sysauth$ connect by grantee#=prior privilege# and privilege#>0 start with grantee#=:1 and privilege#>0
_sql_text_map.63gwvy9mhju8k: SELECT /*+ ORDERED USE_NL(PROCESSSKU SKU) INDEX (PROCESSSKU PROCESSSKU_PERF) INDEX (SKU SKU_PK) INDEX (LOC LOC_PK) */ /* SKUBatchRetrievalHint */ sku.item item, sku.loc loc, replentype , (case when sku.ohpost > to_date('01/02/1970', 'mm/dd/yyyy') then sku.ohpost else loc.ohpost end) trueohpost FROM processsku, SKU sku, LOC loc WHERE sku.item = processsku.item and sku.loc = processsku.loc AND processsku.loc = loc.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2
_sql_text_map.6jb71hzd26m1u: SELECT /* SafetyStockPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc, sspresentation.displayqty, sspresentation.dmdgroup, sspresentation.presentationqty FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC
_sql_text_map.7j17vjtnkzn98: SELECT /* TimePhasedProfileRetrievalHint */ DISTINCT spp.stockoutlvl as stockOutLevel, spp.item as item, spp.loc as loc FROM processsku, SKUPlanningParam spp WHERE processsku.item = spp.item and processsku.loc = spp.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3
_sql_text_map.865qwpcdyggkk: select spare6 from user$ where user#=:1
_sql_text_map.8xgs7k1v2db52: SELECT /*+ LEADING(PROCESSSKU) USE_NL(vehicleload vehicleloadline) INDEX(processsku PROCESSSKU_PERF) */ /* IntransOutTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.source, vehicleloadline.schedshipdate, vehicleload.shipdate, vehicleloadline.expdate, vehicleload.sourcestatus, vehicleloadline.actionallowedsw, vehicleloadline.dmdexpdate, vehicleloadline.primaryitem, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.reviseddate, vehicleloadline.revisedexpdate, vehicleloadline.revisedshipdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.SOURCE=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.source, vehicleloadline.schedshipdate, vehicleload.shipdate, vehicleloadline.exp date ASC
_sql_text_map.9zg9qd9bm4spu: update user$ set spare6=DECODE(to_char(:2, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), '0000-00-00', to_date(NULL), :2) where user#=:1
_sql_text_map.a6tydqr67a7nu: SELECT SID, SERIAL#, SYS_CONTEXT('userenv', 'instance') FROM V$SESSION WHERE USERNAME = UPPER(:B2 ) AND AUDSID = TO_NUMBER(:B1 )

_sql_text_map.awh9nk7p0r3v0: SELECT /*+ LEADING(PROCESSSKU) USE_NL(vehicleload vehicleloadline) INDEX(processsku PROCESSSKU_PERF) */ /* IntransInTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate, vehicleload.deststatus, vehicleload.sourcestatus, vehicleloadline.actionallowedsw, vehicleloadline.expdate, vehicleloadline.primaryitem, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.reviseddate, vehicleloadline.revisedexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate ASC

2h7u1gwu558r7.fullText: INSERT INTO PROCESSSKU ( PROCESSID, ITEM, LOC, ITEMLEVEL, SKULEVEL, NETCHGSW, BATCHNUM ) VALUES ( :B4 , :B1 , :B2 , 0, 0, 1, :B3 )

2qjq3d3u54ajq.fullText: SELECT 1 FROM GV$SESSION A WHERE SID = :B3 AND SERIAL# = :B2 AND INST_ID = :B1 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM MANUGISTICS_SCHEMA B WHERE A.USERNAME = B.SCHEMA_NAME AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYS' AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME <> 'JDA_SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME IS NOT NULL)

4pp47nzdd67qr.fullText: SELECT /* SafetyStockMaxPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC

4qguf81v4r5mx.fullText: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B4 , STOP_REQUEST_DATE = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), NODE_STATE = :B3 WHERE NODE_STATE = :B2 AND ROWNUM = 1 AND SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID = :B1 RETURNING NODE_ID INTO :O0

5dqz0hqtp9fru.fullText: select /*+ connect_by_filtering index(sysauth$ i_sysauth1) */ privilege#, bitand(nvl(option$, 0), 72), grantee#, level from sysauth$ connect by grantee#=prior privilege# and privilege#>0 start with grantee#=:1 and privilege#>0

6jb71hzd26m1u.fullText: SELECT /* SafetyStockPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc, sspresentation.displayqty, sspresentation.dmdgroup, sspresentation.presentationqty FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC

7j17vjtnkzn98.fullText: SELECT /* TimePhasedProfileRetrievalHint */ DISTINCT spp.stockoutlvl as stockOutLevel, spp.item as item, spp.loc as loc FROM processsku, SKUPlanningParam spp WHERE processsku.item = spp.item and processsku.loc = spp.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3

865qwpcdyggkk.fullText: select spare6 from user$ where user#=:1

9huj1akp5b757.fullText: SELECT /*+ ORDERED USE_NL(PROCESSSKU SKU) INDEX (PROCESSSKU PROCESSSKU_BATCH) INDEX (SKU SKU_PK) INDEX (LOC LOC_PK) */ /* SKUBatchRetrievalHint */ sku.item item, sku.loc loc, replentype , (case when sku.ohpost > to_date('01/02/1970', 'mm/dd/yyyy') then sku.ohpost else loc.ohpost end) trueohpost FROM processsku, SKU sku, LOC loc WHERE sku.item = processsku.item and sku.loc = processsku.loc AND processsku.loc = loc.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2

9zg9qd9bm4spu.fullText: update user$ set spare6=DECODE(to_char(:2, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), '0000-00-00', to_date(NULL), :2) where user#=:1

a6tydqr67a7nu.fullText: SELECT SID, SERIAL#, SYS_CONTEXT('userenv', 'instance') FROM V$SESSION WHERE USERNAME = UPPER(:B2 ) AND AUDSID = TO_NUMBER(:B1 )

_sql_text_map.2h7u1gwu558r7: INSERT INTO PROCESSSKU ( PROCESSID, ITEM, LOC, ITEMLEVEL, SKULEVEL, NETCHGSW, BATCHNUM ) VALUES ( :B4 , :B1 , :B2 , 0, 0, 1, :B3 )
_sql_text_map.2qjq3d3u54ajq: SELECT 1 FROM GV$SESSION A WHERE SID = :B3 AND SERIAL# = :B2 AND INST_ID = :B1 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM MANUGISTICS_SCHEMA B WHERE A.USERNAME = B.SCHEMA_NAME AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYS' AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME <> 'JDA_SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME IS NOT NULL)
_sql_text_map.4pp47nzdd67qr: SELECT /* SafetyStockMaxPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC
_sql_text_map.4qguf81v4r5mx: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B4 , STOP_REQUEST_DATE = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), NODE_STATE = :B3 WHERE NODE_STATE = :B2 AND ROWNUM = 1 AND SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID = :B1 RETURNING NODE_ID INTO :O0
_sql_text_map.5dqz0hqtp9fru: select /*+ connect_by_filtering index(sysauth$ i_sysauth1) */ privilege#, bitand(nvl(option$, 0), 72), grantee#, level from sysauth$ connect by grantee#=prior privilege# and privilege#>0 start with grantee#=:1 and privilege#>0
_sql_text_map.6jb71hzd26m1u: SELECT /* SafetyStockPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc, sspresentation.displayqty, sspresentation.dmdgroup, sspresentation.presentationqty FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC
_sql_text_map.7j17vjtnkzn98: SELECT /* TimePhasedProfileRetrievalHint */ DISTINCT spp.stockoutlvl as stockOutLevel, spp.item as item, spp.loc as loc FROM processsku, SKUPlanningParam spp WHERE processsku.item = spp.item and processsku.loc = spp.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3
_sql_text_map.865qwpcdyggkk: select spare6 from user$ where user#=:1
_sql_text_map.9huj1akp5b757: SELECT /*+ ORDERED USE_NL(PROCESSSKU SKU) INDEX (PROCESSSKU PROCESSSKU_BATCH) INDEX (SKU SKU_PK) INDEX (LOC LOC_PK) */ /* SKUBatchRetrievalHint */ sku.item item, sku.loc loc, replentype , (case when sku.ohpost > to_date('01/02/1970', 'mm/dd/yyyy') then sku.ohpost else loc.ohpost end) trueohpost FROM processsku, SKU sku, LOC loc WHERE sku.item = processsku.item and sku.loc = processsku.loc AND processsku.loc = loc.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2
_sql_text_map.9zg9qd9bm4spu: update user$ set spare6=DECODE(to_char(:2, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), '0000-00-00', to_date(NULL), :2) where user#=:1
_sql_text_map.a6tydqr67a7nu: SELECT SID, SERIAL#, SYS_CONTEXT('userenv', 'instance') FROM V$SESSION WHERE USERNAME = UPPER(:B2 ) AND AUDSID = TO_NUMBER(:B1 )

_sql_text_map.awh9nk7p0r3v0: SELECT /*+ LEADING(PROCESSSKU) USE_NL(vehicleload vehicleloadline) INDEX(processsku PROCESSSKU_PERF) */ /* IntransInTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate, vehicleload.deststatus, vehicleload.sourcestatus, vehicleloadline.actionallowedsw, vehicleloadline.expdate, vehicleloadline.primaryitem, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.reviseddate, vehicleloadline.revisedexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate ASC

_sql_text_map.cg1ukabw429d9: SELECT /* PlanArrivTVQHint */ planarriv.item, planarriv.dest, planarriv.schedarrivdate, planarriv.expdate, planarriv.actionallowedsw, planarriv.firmplansw, planarriv.qty, planarriv.reviseddate, planarriv.revisedexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, PLANARRIV WHERE ( PLANARRIV.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND PLANARRIV.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY planarriv.item, planarriv.dest, planarriv.schedarrivdate, planarriv.expdate ASC
_sql_text_map.cxv29ppvmztwv: SELECT /* IntransInProxyTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE (( vehicleload.deststatus != 3 and vehicleloadline.primaryitem != vehicleloadline.item)) AND ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate ASC
_sql_text_map.d68fgmq7cuhp7: delete /* SkuStatStaticPurgeHint */ from SKUSTATSTATIC where (item, loc) in (select item, loc from ProcessSku where ProcessID = :1 and batchnum = :2 )
